_description: Parser regression corpus for tricky issuer/form combinations. Each entry documents a known parsing challenge with expected section structure. Use in tests to verify parser changes don't regress on hard cases.

cases.description: Standard 10-K with well-structured sections. Baseline reference.
Foreign private issuer 20-F with non-standard section numbering (uses 'Item' numbering different from 10-K).
known_issues: Some sections use different names than US domestic filers

cases.description: Korean issuer 6-K. Minimal structured sections, often a single exhibit.
known_issues: 6-K filings often lack standard section structure
Content may be a single press release attachment

cases.description: Large 10-K with extensive risk factors and complex financial statements.
known_issues: Very long Risk Factors section (>15K words)

cases.description: Dutch company 20-F with EU-style reporting structure.
known_issues: EU reporting conventions may differ from US section names

cases.description: Amended 10-K (10-K/A). Tests that amendment suffix stripping works correctly.
known_issues: 10-K/A may have fewer sections than original
Some sections may reference the original filing

cases.description: Current report (8-K). Typically has few sections, mostly Item references.
known_issues: Often just 1-2 items with brief content

cases.description: Schedule 13D ownership filing. Non-standard structure for narrative parsing.
known_issues: SC 13D has a tabular structure, not narrative sections
Parser should not expect standard section names
